decisions.rationale: AC-1 explicitly requires optional segment labels via slots or props; Bubble bTIJL is a style-injection HTML node with no visible axis-label copy in drsam-99657.bubble. The optional labels[] row is an intentional SPA reuse extension aligned with decompose scope ('shared across journal and check-in surfaces'), not a missing ui_ref. criteria_audit AC-1 passes; no downstream depends_on gate requires IR-bound label copy at this layer.

bTIJL is sr-only style scaffolding (aria-hidden, dangerouslySetInnerHTML); IR parent_id is bTIHr and the node is present with correct data-bubble-id and preserved static style HTML. AC-3 scopes bTIJL to static copy/formatting preservation and dynamic width in React — both satisfied. Nesting inside the segment flex row vs Bubble sibling order after segment groups does not affect visual output, a11y, or downstream journal/check-in mounts; review ui_evidence already passes bTIJL. Precedent: DS-02 triage accepted equivalent DOM-structure deviations when named ui_refs pass and ACs are met.

summary: 0 fix_now, 0 defer, 2 accept. Advance OK — re-review ok=true, coverage 100%, 0 blockers, rewrite_verification resolved (B-1/C-1 bTIIh canonical id). C-2 accepted: optional labels[] is AC-1-scoped reuse ergonomics, not an IR fidelity gap. C-3 accepted: sr-only bTIJL structural nesting is AC-compliant and visually equivalent.
